---
title: "SASE Modernization Validation Suite"
subtitle: "Conformance tests and validation criteria for the sase domain"
doc-type: domain-validation-suite
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Adapter Owner]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
domain: sase
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/sase/sase.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# SASE Modernization Validation Suite

## Scope

Conformance tests and validation criteria for the SASE domain. Pairs
with the
[SASE Modernization Spec](../../../modernization-specs/sase/sase.qmd).
The suite gates whether a SASE deployment meets substrate conformance
— primary focus is identity-derived access, certificate-anchored
overlay, and decision-record emission.

[IMAGE-01: SASE validation suite diagram. Center shows a SASE PDP/PEP node. Inputs from the left: Identity Claim (from identity plane), Resource Scope, Certificate Thumbprint, Policy Refs. Output to the right: SASE decision record (permit / deny / escalate) with full provenance envelope. Above the PDP, a small "Conformance gates" badge bar lists the four test categories. Below, a footer band reads "SASE = identity-driven, certificate-anchored, decision-recorded". Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.]

## Test categories

### 1. Identity-derived access conformance

- Every SASE access decision consumes an identity claim as input.
- Decisions on resources without identity context emit a
  `DRIFT-IDENTITY` finding rather than a default-permit.
- SASE PDPs do not maintain a parallel identity store.

### 2. Certificate-anchored overlay

- Overlay certificates are issued bound to identity-plane objects.
- Decision records carry `certificate_thumbprint` matching the
  identity context.
- Certificate retirement triggers `DRIFT-IDENTITY` for any active
  decisions still bound to the retired cert.

### 3. Decision-record schema

For each SASE decision:

- `pdp_id`, `decision`, `identity_context`, `resource_scope`,
  `network_overlay`, `certificate_thumbprint`, `policy_refs`,
  `evaluation_timestamp` are all present.
- `decision` is one of `permit | deny | escalate`.
- Full provenance envelope wraps the decision record.

### 4. Drift detection coverage

- `DRIFT-IDENTITY` — identity claim cannot resolve.
- `DRIFT-AUTHZ` — decision recorded outside consent envelope.
- `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` — overlay certificate fails to bind to
  identity-plane object.
- `DRIFT-SCHEMA` — vendor policy schema diverges from canonical
  shape.

## Evidence expectations

- Per-decision claim records with full provenance.
- Certificate-to-identity binding evidence.
- OSCAL artifacts mapping SASE decisions onto NIST 800-53 AC, SC, IA
  controls.
- Boundary-disposition evidence where SASE vendor features are
  unavailable in GCC-Moderate.

## Conformance gates

| Gate | Required | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **T1 — identity-derived access** | Decisions consume identity claims | Required |
| **T2 — certificate-anchored overlay** | Cert-to-identity binding present | Required |
| **T3 — decision schema** | Full decision-record schema validates | Required |
| **T4 — drift coverage** | All four SASE-relevant drift classes detected | Target |

## Drift procedures

`DRIFT-IDENTITY` (P1) and `DRIFT-AUTHZ` (P1/P2) dominate SASE drift
operations. Both halt the affected access flow and alert. Other
classes auto-remediate when deterministic; otherwise escalate.

## Honest limits

- SASE vendor maturity in GCC-Moderate varies; T1–T3 conformance
  requires either an in-boundary vendor or a compensating-mechanism
  declaration.
- Certificate-anchored overlay requires agency PKI capable of issuing
  identity-bound certificates; programs without that PKI cannot
  fully realize T2.
- Some SASE vendor PDPs do not emit decision records natively; the
  adapter may have to synthesize records from logs, with explicit
  `source_classification: synthesized`.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Spec — SASE](../../../modernization-specs/sase/sase.qmd)
- [Architecture Series — Six-Plane Architecture](../../../architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd)
- [Modernization Registry (canon)](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml)
